title: Fix inconsistent world_to_pixel in SlicedLowLevelWCS
description:
Fixes astropy/astropy#13579.

When a 3D FITS WCS has a non-trivial PCij matrix coupling spatial and spectral axes, slicing one wavelength out with SlicedLowLevelWCS and calling world_to_pixel on the spatial coordinate returns an incorrect pixel value for one spatial dimension.

Root cause: SlicedLowLevelWCS.world_to_pixel_values in astropy/wcs/wcsapi/wrappers/sliced_wcs.py reconstructs full world values for the underlying WCS by filling dropped world dimensions with a hard-coded placeholder 1.0. For a sliced spectral axis in meters, 1.0 is astronomically far from the actual wavelength (~1e-10 m), and the non-linear/PC-coupled inversion returns wrong spatial pixels.

Fix: fill dropped world dimensions from self.dropped_world_dimensions['value'] instead of 1.0. Add a regression test in astropy/wcs/wcsapi/wrappers/tests/test_sliced_wcs.py with a correlated spectral/spatial PC matrix and assert consistency between full and sliced WCS, plus round-trip consistency.
